claim: For all integers n > 1, n is prime if and only if (n-1)! + 1 is divisible by n.
predicate: (n <= 1) or (is_prime(n) == ((factorial(n-1) + 1) % n == 0))

claim: If n is prime and n > 5, then n divides (n-2)! - 1.
predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or (n <= 5) or ((factorial(n-2) - 1) % n == 0)

claim: If n is prime and n > 3, then (n-1)! ≡ -1 mod n^2 fails for n=5 but holds for n=7.
predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or (n <= 3) or ((n == 5 and (factorial(n-1) + 1) % (n*n) != 0) or (n == 7 and (factorial(n-1) + 1) % (n*n) == 0))

claim: If n is prime and n > 5, then n divides both (n-1)! + 1 and (n-2)! - 1.
predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or (n <= 5) or (((factorial(n-1) + 1) % n == 0) and ((factorial(n-2) - 1) % n == 0))
